page: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv5 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, this instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The server includes 154 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate single-core capability but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall within the average range, whereas multi-core CPU metrics, including Passmark and multi-threaded Geekbench tests, rank in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance for both small cached and large uncached blocks, though the overall Passmark memory score remains average. Cryptographic operations perform moderately well, utilizing hardware-accelerated instructions like AES and SHA-NI.

Given its high memory density relative to processing power, the E4ds_v5 is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that do not demand heavy parallel CPU throughput. The presence of 154 GB of local storage makes it suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. However, its low multi-threaded performance and weak Redis SET throughput suggest it is less optimal for high-concurrency web serving or compute-heavy parallel processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, it features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and 154 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance alongside weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. The instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that require a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio but do not demand intensive parallel computation. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and enterprise applications with moderate processing requirements.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated Intel processors and local SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
